title: Weekly Mazars Comment The Risk Of A Financial Accident Is Being Removed
section_path: Weekly Mazars Comment The Risk Of A Financial Accident Is Being Removed
section_path_text: Weekly Mazars Comment The Risk Of A Financial Accident Is Being Removed
summary:
The risk of a financial accident is being removed
Summary
One of the key risks we persistently flagged in the second part of last year has been the swiftly rising risks in the bond market. The Fed performed the fastest rate hike in the last forty years, against a bond market that never expected inflation to stage a comeback or rates to take off for at least a generation. Yet, since the beginning of the year, rates began to climb down and credit spreads tightened as markets anticipated that weaker inflation data would eventually lead the Fed to take its foot off the pedal.
